---
title: ""
---

:::: {style="display:flex; align-items:center; gap:60px; margin-top:60px;"}
<img src="images/profile.jpg" class="profile-image">

::: {style="max-width:520px;"}
## Natalia Cabrera-Morales

I am a Chilean [PhD candidate](https://www.crim.cam.ac.uk/people/natalia-cabrera-morales) in Criminology at University of Cambridge. I am also a lawyer and hold a master's in Sociology and an Mphil in Criminological Research. 

:::
::::

I graduated from law in the top 10% of the cohort. I was the second best student of the MPhil in Criminological Research (2023-2024), being appointed as Manuel López-Rey proxime accesserunt, and an Emily Davis Scholar. I topped several classes during my Masters in Sociology, but was at the bottom of the class for quantitative methods. I enjoy challenges so I kept learning about them and now I teach and use them daily. 

I studied my MPhil with a full scholarship from ANID-Cambridge trust and I am fully funded by Cambridge Trust for my PhD.

My [PhD](phd.qmd) focuses on police misconduct but I am generally interested in criminal justice institutions. My PhD supervisor is [Dr. Charles Lanfear](https://clanfear.github.io/).

My previous and side [projects](research.qmd) have involved gender and crime against women too. 

I am passionate about methodology, I have experience in practice and teaching with both quantitative and qualitative methods. I programme in R and Python; I am mostly a self-taught programmer. However, one of my favourite academic activities is [teaching](teaching.qmd). I am also a [person](me.qmd).


------------------------------------------------------------------------
